solutions.solution: As J list precedes K, J cannot be earlier than fifth, so M could not immediately follow K. That the others could follow K, is shown by the following schedules: 1 2 3 4 5 6 (1) J K L M N O (2) J K L N M O (4) J K N L M O (5) J K O N L M

If we consider the additional information on a diagram: 1 2 3 4 5 6 K N L M O Next, we have already determined that M cannot be earlier than fifth: 1 2 3 4 5 6 K N L M O Next, since J must precede N, there are three possibilities: 1 2 3 4 5 6 J K N L M O J K L N M O J K O N L M (1) describes one of the three possible schedules. The other choices do not describe one of the three possible schedules.
Q. 56-61: See....... (1) to (6) can be summarised as below P V Sec or Sec VP....... (2) S U....... (3)
